laws.title: Florida Minimum Wage — $14.00/hr (September 2025)
laws.summary: Florida's minimum wage increases to $14.00/hour on September 30, 2025 (it increases each September 30 per Amendment 2). It will reach $15.00/hour by September 30, 2026. Tipped employees: $10.98/hr minimum (with tip credit). Florida does not have a state overtime law — federal FLSA applies.
compliance_actions: Plan for $14.00/hr minimum wage for all Florida employees effective September 30, 2025
Update tipped employee pay to at least $10.98/hr on September 30, 2025
Post the updated Florida minimum wage notice

laws.title: Florida E-Verify Requirement for Employers
laws.summary: Florida law (SB 1718) requires private employers with 25+ employees to use E-Verify for all new hires effective July 1, 2023. Employers must verify new employees within 3 business days of hire. Non-compliance can result in fines and loss of state licenses. Employers should retain I-9 forms AND E-Verify case numbers.
compliance_actions: Enroll in E-Verify at e-verify.uscis.gov if you have 25+ Florida employees
Run E-Verify for all new hires in Florida within 3 business days of their start date
Retain E-Verify case numbers with your I ¹ €Rd TT XTeGoogle Sheet for state changes
